stem: A application enters the immigration pipeline. The probability of passing document verification is 98%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing background check is 76%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing interview is between 58% and 68% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). What is the overall probability that the application is approved?

ground_truth.answer: P(approved) ranges from 0.432 to 0.506 as the interview pass rate varies from 58% to 68%. Point estimate: 0.469.

solution_steps: The application must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the interview stage creates a 7.4 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the interview rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
